name: Workbench Semantic Projection Proof Gate
purpose: Produce direct Module-owned Evidence that Kernel and Workbench projections preserve exact authoring and action semantics without adapter-side governance compilation.

oracle.description: The fixed Change Kernel Workbench semantics test entrypoint must exist, prove the Kernel-owned projection, and exit zero.

rejects: selectable Claims outside a primary Module's owned or dependency Contracts
omitted, duplicated, or invented authoring Claims or acceptance-eligible routes
authoring routes that differ from the current Architecture Profile route product
Change Gate actions derived from current browser Project facts instead of the frozen Governance Baseline, Kernel lifecycle selection, and compiler-owned Claim-route annotations
omitted, duplicated, or invented existing-Change Gate actions
unavailable actions with an empty, duplicate, unknown, or out-of-precedence disabled-reason code list

residualUncertainty: Local Workbench projection and real adapter adoption are proved by a separate Module-owned command.
Browser DOM interaction remains outside this Gate and belongs to LGT-014.

oracle.description: The fixed Local Workbench profile test entrypoint must prove pure projection of Kernel semantics and exit zero.

rejects: loss or invention of selectable Claims, exact Gate and command routes, enabled state, or declared workbenchDisabledReasonCodes
omission or duplication of canonical Kernel authoring or action facts
CLI, HTTP, or browser adapter reconstruction of Contract visibility, Gate applicability, or lifecycle policy
aggregate score, percentage, confidence, health, readiness, or green-light conclusions
top-level view payloads containing source bodies, package bodies, Evidence bodies, stdout, or stderr instead of bounded references and digests

residualUncertainty: Browser DOM rendering and interaction remain outside this Gate and belong to LGT-014.
Cross-workspace Accepted history portability remains an explicit Knowledge Gap.
